metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=incapable driver=ready — vibe has a real plan mode (exit_plan_mode approval gate → accept-edits/implement) but the arc's defining working→waiting episode is never flushed to messages.jsonl during the approval pause (route frozen).

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready

Route: frozen. This cell is instantiation (b) of architect-editor-pair — Mistral Vibe's plan→implement MODE pair (one model) captured across the approval gate, the FULL lifecycle arc `ready → working → waiting → working → ready` (NOT the Aider two-models-in-one-turn shape). Its load-bearing, defining observable is the mid-arc `working → waiting` episode driven by the plan-approval pause, plus the post-approval `waiting → working → ready` continuation into a real edit. Vibe genuinely raises that approval prompt, but the `→ waiting` transition produces ZERO trace in the only transport the daemon tails — messages.jsonl — so no recording could verify the arc. This is the SAME architectural blocker vibe's `user-blocking-plan-mode-approval` (2.18) cell established; this cell is its full-arc superset, and the very signal 2.18 proved vibe cannot surface is the one this arc hinges on.

## Two instantiations, and which one this is

The architect/editor essence is: a planning/architect contribution produces a plan, then an editing/editor contribution applies the edits. Two instantiations: (a) two MODELS in one turn (Aider `--architect`), and (b) a plan→implement MODE pair across the approval gate (Claude Code plan mode; the broadened meaning the canonical claudecode cell instantiates). The 5.4 description pins this cell to instantiation (b), one model throughout.

Vibe cannot produce instantiation (a): it uses ONE model per turn. `active_model` is a single alias and `get_active_model()` resolves exactly one `ModelConfig` from the `models` catalog (`config/vibe_schema.py:88,92,306-312`); the `models` list is a selectable pool (mistral-medium-3.5 / devstral-small / local aliases), not an architect+editor dual-model orchestration. A keyword sweep of the whole `vibe/` tree for `architect` / `editor_model` / `architect_model` / `two_model` / `multi_model` returned no orchestration hits — vibe has no within-turn two-model split, and the sidecar (parser.go's meta.json read) carries a single `active_model`. Instantiation (a) is therefore not producible — but it is not this cell.

## Why agent_supports is yes (instantiation b)

Mistral Vibe 2.19.0 ships a genuine plan mode with a plan→implement mode pair:
- A builtin `PLAN` agent profile (`BuiltinAgentName.PLAN = "plan"`, `core/agents/models.py:41,117`) and an `ACCEPT_EDITS = "accept-edits"` profile (`models.py:42,132`) — the architect (plan) and editor (implement) modes of the pair.
- A builtin `exit_plan_mode` tool (`core/tools/builtins/exit_plan_mode.py`) that blocks on the user's approval and, on approval, switches from plan mode to accept-edits mode — the mid-arc gate that continues PAST approval into the implement phase.
- Plan-mode middleware injects a read-only-enforcing system prompt and instructs the model to "call the exit_plan_mode tool to request user approval and switch to implementation mode" (`core/middleware.py:151-182`).

So the agent deterministically performs the plan-propose → approve → implement mode-pair behavior the scenario names, continuing past the gate into a real edit → agent=yes.

## Why daemon_capability is incapable

The vibe adapter is a transcript-only Source — `agent.FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.vibe/logs/session/}` tailing each session's `messages.jsonl` (agent.go), with a read-only `transcripts` observe permission and NO hook/permission transport. The defining `working → waiting` episode of this arc cannot form on disk:

1. Vibe flushes messages.jsonl per-TURN, not per-message. In the turn loop `act()`, `_save_messages()` (`agent_loop.py:1076`, → `session_logger.save_interaction` at `agent_loop.py:741-742`) runs only AFTER `_perform_llm_turn()` fully completes — and `_perform_llm_turn` is what executes the tool calls, inside which `exit_plan_mode.run()` blocks on the user's approval. The only other turn-adjacent save is in `inject_user_context` (`agent_loop.py:777`), not during tool execution. So the assistant `exit_plan_mode` tool_call and its `role:tool` result are BOTH written to disk together, only AFTER the user has already resolved the approval. During the entire approval-pause window messages.jsonl gains nothing — the open-tool state exists only in the in-memory message list. The `→ waiting` transition that defines this scenario is never persisted to the transport the daemon tails.

2. Even if it were persisted, the daemon's two paths to `waiting` do not fire: rule 0 `PermissionPending → waiting` is Claude-Code-permission-hook-only (vibe fires no hook), and rule 1 `NeedsUserAttention` requires an OPEN tool whose name is in `isUserBlockingToolName` = `AskUserQuestion` / `ExitPlanMode` (CamelCase) / `question` (`core/pkg/tailer/tailer_config.go:34-37`; `state_classifier.go`). Vibe's tool is `exit_plan_mode` (snake_case, passed through by parser.go's parseToolCalls) and is absent from that set. This is a secondary gap; the load-bearing blocker is the per-turn flush cadence.

Without the gating `waiting` transition the post-approval `waiting → working → ready` continuation is unreachable, so the full `ready → working → waiting → working → ready` arc could never form on disk → daemon=incapable (not bug: there is no on-disk trace to mis-handle).

## Why driver_capability is ready

The driver is not the blocker (moot under a frozen route). Plan-mode entry is available as the `--agent plan` launch flag, so engaging plan mode is a setup precondition rather than a missing runtime step primitive; the elicitation itself (send a prompt, let the model call exit_plan_mode, approve, continue) is within the freshly-scaffolded send/slash/sleep set. The blocker is correctly isolated to architecture (no transport trace), not tooling → route frozen, not driver-gap. (A runtime toggle via Shift+Tab would need a `keys` step the scaffolded driver lacks, but that path is not required since `--agent plan` sets the mode at launch.)

## What would change this

Either (a) vibe begins flushing messages.jsonl mid-turn — writing the assistant `exit_plan_mode` tool_call to disk BEFORE the approval block resolves, leaving an observable open-tool window — AND `exit_plan_mode` is added to the daemon's user-blocking tool set; or (b) a vibe permission-hook transport analogous to Claude Code's feeds `PermissionPending` directly. Neither exists in 2.19.0. At that point the plan→approve→implement mode-pair recipe (`--agent plan` launch, send the plan task, approve the gate, wait for the implement turn + a real edit) would mirror the claudecode reference.
caveats: Provisional on the EMISSION axis: the verdict that vibe never writes the open exit_plan_mode tool_call to messages.jsonl during the approval pause rests on the 2.19.0 source call-graph (act()'s only turn-loop flush is _save_messages at agent_loop.py:1076, after the blocking _perform_llm_turn/tool execution completes; the earlier save at agent_loop.py:777 is in inject_user_context, not the turn loop). A live recording driving genuine plan mode is the only thing that could promote this from provisional to settled — hence confidence 0.82, matching the vibe 2.18 and gemini-cli 5.4 siblings.

The tool-name mismatch (vibe emits snake_case exit_plan_mode, absent from the daemon's CamelCase isUserBlockingToolName set) is a secondary gap: even if fixed, the per-turn flush cadence means no open-tool window is ever on disk to classify. The emission-timing limit is the load-bearing reason.

Instantiation (a) two-models-in-one-turn is separately impossible for vibe (single active_model; get_active_model returns one ModelConfig; no architect/editor orchestration in source), but the 5.4 description pins this cell to instantiation (b) — the one-model plan→implement mode pair — so the verdict turns on the mode-pair arc, not the two-model shape.

sources.note: Sibling 2.18 cell: agent=yes daemon=incapable (conf 0.82) — vibe's exit_plan_mode approval gate → waiting is never flushed to messages.jsonl (per-turn flush at agent_loop.py:1076). This 5.4 cell is 2.18's full-arc superset and inherits the identical blocker on the gating working→waiting transition.

vibe 2.19.0 flush cadence: act()'s turn loop calls _save_messages() only at line 1076 AFTER _perform_llm_turn() (which runs the blocking exit_plan_mode approval) completes; _save_messages→session_logger.save_interaction at 741-742; the only other save (777) is in inject_user_context, not during tool execution. So the open exit_plan_mode tool_call is never on disk during the approval pause → the working→waiting episode leaves zero trace.

BuiltinAgentName.PLAN="plan" (line 41) and ACCEPT_EDITS="accept-edits" (line 42), with PLAN AgentProfile at line 117 — the architect (plan) and editor (implement/accept-edits) modes of the pair. Confirms agent=yes for instantiation (b).

Builtin exit_plan_mode tool that requests user approval and switches plan→accept-edits (implement) mode; middleware.py:151-182 instructs the model to call it 'to request user approval and switch to implementation mode'. The mid-arc gate the scenario continues PAST — but its approval pause is never persisted.

Single-model per turn: active_model is one alias (line 88); models is a selectable catalog (line 92); get_active_model() returns exactly one ModelConfig (306-312). No architect/editor dual-model orchestration (0 hits sweeping vibe/ for architect|editor_model|two_model|multi_model) — instantiation (a) two-models-in-one-turn is not producible; this cell is instantiation (b).

vibe parser maps only user_message / assistant_message(working) / turn_done(ready) / tool_result; no waiting-state path. exit_plan_mode is passed through snake_case via parseToolCalls (nested function.name). No parser path derives a pending/awaiting/held-open signal → grounds daemon=incapable.

isUserBlockingToolName = AskUserQuestion / ExitPlanMode (CamelCase) / question (lines 34-37); vibe's snake_case exit_plan_mode is absent, so even an open call could not drive NeedsUserAttention rule 1. Secondary to the flush-cadence blocker.

Cross-agent sibling: gemini-cli 5.4 is agent=yes daemon=incapable frozen (conf 0.82) for the identical reason — the plan-approval waiting episode is never persisted to the tailed JSONL. vibe's transport architecture matches; same verdict and framing.

recipe.notes: Route frozen (daemon=incapable). No recording is possible: the mid-arc working→waiting transition driven by the exit_plan_mode approval pause is never written to messages.jsonl (vibe flushes per-turn at agent_loop.py:1076, only after the blocking tool execution completes), so the full ready→working→waiting→working→ready arc could not form on disk and no recipe could elicit a verifiable observation. Would become recordable only if vibe flushes the open tool_call mid-turn AND exit_plan_mode is added to the daemon's user-blocking tool set (or vibe grows a permission-hook transport feeding PermissionPending) — at which point the plan→approve→implement mode-pair recipe (--agent plan launch, send the plan task, approve the gate, wait for the implement turn + real edit) would mirror the claudecode reference.
